english: to form a line; to queue up

etymology.description: 排(排) (pái, "arrange; line up") + 隊(隊) (duì, "team; line; rank")
etymology.interpretation: 排(排) (pái, "arrange; line up") denotes the action of arranging or aligning items or people. 隊(隊) (duì, "team; line; rank") refers to a collection of people or objects organized into a sequence or formation. Combining them, 排队(排隊) (páiduì) means organizing individuals into a line, emphasizing orderly arrangement—a queue or process of lining up people.

examples.chinese: 大家在商店前排队(大家在商店前排隊)。
examples.english: Everyone is lining up in front of the store.

examples.chinese: 请有序排队(請有序排隊)，不要拥挤(不要擁擠)。
examples.english: Please queue up in an orderly fashion and do not crowd.

examples.chinese: 他们在售票处排队(他們在售票處排隊)，等待买票(等待買票)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài shòupiàochù páiduì, děngdài mǎipiào.
examples.english: They are queuing at the ticket office, waiting to buy tickets.
